channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate confirms Trump nominee Caine for chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff in overnight vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff on Friday, filling the position almost two months after President Donald Trump fired his predecessor. Trump nominated Caine to become the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration to refer Maine to Justice Department over transgender participation in sports
item.description: The deadline has arrived for Maine officials to reach a resolution with the U.S. Education Department over a finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing transgender athletes to participate in girls’ sports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump announces picks for ambassadorships to combat antisemitism and promote religious freedom
item.description: President Donald Trump announced a series of nominations and appointments, including one to combat antisemitism, in a series of posts on Truth Social Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink to step down amid high-stakes war diplomacy
item.description: Biden-appointed Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink is preparing to step down from her post less than three years on the job at a pivotal moment in the Ukraine-Russia war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese officials claimed behind closed doors PRC played role in US cyberattacks: report
item.description: Chinese officials reportedly acknowledged the PRC's role in cyberattacks on U.S. infrastructure that occurred over the past few years, during a secret meeting in December.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump admin must bring Kilmar Abrego Garcia back to US
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court upheld on Thursday a judge's order requiring President Donald Trump's administration to facilitate the return to the United States of a Salvadoran man who the government has acknowledged was deported in error to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: By John Kruzel and Andrew Chung / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says immigrants can return legally but they must self-deport now, floats potential 60-day deadline
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday there will soon be a deadline, likely "60 days," by which illegal immigrants must be gone from the U.S., or they will not be allowed to try to reenter legally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump admin wants to put Columbia under federal oversight to ensure compliance with antisemitism policies
item.description: The Trump administration is reportedly pursuing a legal arrangement that would allow the federal government to enforce Columbia's compliance with its policies on antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red states get in line with Trump's DEI ban in schools as compliance deadline nears
item.description: Trump's Department of Education gave public schools, universities until April 24 to certify compliance with his federal mandate to strip all DEI policies or face federal funding cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US budget deficit grows to $1.3 trillion, the second highest six-month level on record
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. budget deficit has grown to more than $1.3 trillion in the first half of the 2025 fiscal year — the second highest six-month deficit on record, according to Treasury Department data released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China targets Hollywood films in retaliation of tariffs; Trump responds
item.description: China retaliated against U.S.-imposed tariffs on Thursday by reducing the number of allowed American made movies that can be shown in the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department seeks death penalty for man who killed fellow supermax inmate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department will seek the death penalty for a man accused of killing a fellow prisoner at the federal government’s “Supermax” prison in Florence, Colorado, while serving a life sentence for killing his cellmate at another lockup, the department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department moves to drop case against alleged top MS-13 leader, seeks to deport him instead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has moved to drop a criminal case against the alleged East Coast leader of the MS-13 gang less two weeks after he was charged, saying they plan to deport him instead of prosecuting him in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where did U.S. humanities grants go? To projects from a baseball film to AI research
item.description: From AI research to historical preservation, programs funded by grants from the National Endowment for the Humanities reach every corner of the U.S. Now the government has terminated those grants.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair, MPR News

item.title: As measles spreads, federal budget cuts force closure of vaccine clinics
item.description: Federal funding cuts, though temporarily blocked by a judge, have upended vaccination outreach across the country, including in Arizona, Minnesota, Nevada, Texas, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith, Arielle Zionts, and Jackie Fortiér, MPR News

item.title: Judges bar use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans in NY, TX
item.description: The judges ruled detainees accused of gang affiliations must be granted a hearing to prove otherwise before being deported.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: House passes bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in a move that would vastly diminish the ability of courts to block President Trump’s policies. Dubbed the No Rogue Rulings Act, the legislation from Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Stocks soar after Trump announces 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for some
item.description: Although markets opened mixed on Wednesday, stocks soared after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: China vows retaliation and a 'fight to the end' after Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: Trump said on Truth Social he would impose the new tariffs on China if Beijing did not retract a 34% retaliatory tariff on U.S. goods that it announced in response to Trump's initial salvo last Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers in Nevada jobs? Democrat's legislation opens arms to Trump cast-offs
item.description: The federal government is shrinking, and that might be an opportunity for Nevada state government to find some experienced new employees.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Proposed California bill seeks to extend last call until 4 a.m. in downtown areas
item.description: A new California bill would allow bars and restaurants in designated hospitality zones to serve alcohol until 4 a.m. on certain days, beginning Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid amid President Donald Trump's planned cuts to the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC
